title: hash_sz try_from after earlier len check creates unreachable error branch
description: In key.rs::sign() and key.rs::verify(), hash.len() is validated to be exactly 32 at the top of the function, then a try_from conversion is done: let hash_sz = std::ffi::c_int::try_from(hash.len()).map_err(|_| Error::InvalidArg(...))?. The error branch is dead: 32 always fits in c_int. The comment even says 'hash is validated to be 32 bytes above; 32 fits in c_int on all targets.' The try_from adds noise, an unreachable branch, and implies a runtime check that isn't needed. Rewrite as: let hash_sz = 32_i32; (or hash.len() as c_int with a safety comment).
